name: Chia Seeds
summary: Tiny seeds that swell into a gel — exceptionally high in soluble fiber and the plant omega-3 ALA, studied for steadying blood sugar.
superfoodReason: One of the most fiber- and ALA-dense foods, whose gel-forming soluble fiber has trial evidence for improving glycemic markers.

benefits.mechanism: Chia's soluble fiber absorbs many times its weight in water and forms a viscous gel that slows how fast carbohydrates are digested and glucose enters the blood. A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized trials found chia improved glycemic status (fasting glucose and HbA1c).

citations.title: The effectiveness of chia seed in improving glycemic status: a systematic review and meta-analysis (2024)

benefits.claim: Very high in fiber and plant omega-3 (ALA)
benefits.mechanism: Chia is among the richest whole-food sources of both fiber (which feeds gut bacteria and supports regularity) and the plant omega-3 ALA, plus a useful amount of calcium.

citations.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: Chia can soak up roughly 10–12 times its weight in liquid, turning into a pudding-like gel — the same gel that slows digestion in the gut.

cautions.effect: Chia absorbs many times its weight in water. Eaten dry with too little fluid it can swell on the way down — there is a documented case of it lodging in the oesophagus — and a sudden big jump in fibre brings bloating and cramping.
cautions.threshold: Soak them, or drink plenty alongside; increase the amount gradually.

citations.title: Rheological characterization of chia seed gum as a thickening agent used for dysphagia management (Foods 2024)
